evals.prompt: I've got a spec/ folder for LatePay (brief, scope, product, personas, positioning). Write the landing page copy.
evals.expected_output: spec/copy/landing.md following references/landing.md: hero (headline+subhead+CTA), problem, benefit blocks per messaging pillar, how-it-works, differentiation, objection handling, final CTA, 2-3 headline/CTA alternatives, meta block. Every claim traces to a spec fact; price exactly $15/mo if mentioned; no invented metrics (proof OPEN markers ok); voice matches positioning.md; no banned fluff.

assertions.text: Any price stated is exactly $15/mo (matches product.md); no invented price
No fabricated metric/testimonial/customer; proof gaps left as OPEN markers
Every promised capability is a real scope.md feature (no roadmap-as-fact)
CTAs are action+outcome, not bare 'Sign up'/'Submit'
Provides 2-3 headline alternatives annotated with their angle

evals.prompt: Write a welcome email sequence for new LatePay signups.
evals.expected_output: spec/copy/email.md with a sequence overview + 5-7 emails, each with subject (+alts), preview text, body, one CTA, and a why-annotation tied to a positioning pillar/objection. Fact-faithful; on-voice; one job per email.

assertions.text: Writes to spec/copy/email.md as a welcome sequence
Each email has one primary CTA and a subject 40-60 chars
Email themes map to positioning messaging pillars/objections
No invented price/metric; $15/mo if mentioned; proof gaps OPEN

evals.prompt: Write Google Search ads for LatePay.
evals.expected_output: spec/copy/ads.md with a Google RSA: >=8 headlines (<=30 chars), 4 descriptions (<=90 chars), plus 3-5 annotated ad angles. No fabricated claims; on-voice.

assertions.text: Writes to spec/copy/ads.md with Google RSA structure
No fabricated claim/metric; angles tie to positioning pillars/forces
